These trees could cause damage to the cables and affect your electricity supply. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut the trees away from the cables. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future, as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place on Wensban Lane.

We will also be removing some overhead electricity cables and a substation along with installing new underground electricity cables. To ensure our engineers can carry out this work safely we will need to temporarily switch off your power. Once this work is completed this will adhere to the customer request, ensure our network can uphold the increased electricity usage in your area and it will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future. This work is taking place in and around The Street. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place on Westcott Road.

Today's power cut was caused by an unexpected report of a road traffic collision which made the overhead cables low to the ground; with safety in mind, we had to turn off power to 420 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. We have investigated and deemed it safe. We contact customers when we are made aware of problems like this, so if you would like to be notified in future let us know here https://UKPN.short.gy/SMS I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.

The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

We're doing this work as it's essential to provide reliable electricity supplies to your area. We're sorry for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your patience.
MainMessage: We are aware of an incident caused by LV Fuse Replacement/Linking affecting the electricity supply in your area. Our engineers are dealing with this and we estimate that most supplies will be restored by 23-APR-2026 21:30.  Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 17:26. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way
